title: Parkside Appraisal, Litigation Appraisal, Railroad Billboard Tax Correction
summary: Michael Layman presents: Item 44 -- hiring appraiser for 1939 Fayette St East property in contemplation of sale to Parkside Commons; Councilor Nave notes Parkside has new property management crew as of last month. Item 45 -- independent trial-ready appraisal for ongoing litigation. Item 46 -- tax roll correction for four railroad properties with billboards inadvertently classified as Section 7 (railroad use), returning to Section 1 (fully taxable). Item 47 (executive session on pending settlement) deferred to Monday.
raw_content:
[01:21:47] item 44. The Department of Assessment
[01:21:50] requests the hiring of an appraiser for
[01:21:52] the property at 1939 Fiat Street East to
[01:21:56] Ives um in contemplation of its sale.
[01:22:00] >> And that's all the Parkside.
[01:22:02] >> Uh yes, the applicant to purchase
[01:22:03] Parkside comments.
[01:22:08] >> Thank you.
[01:22:10] Um,
[01:22:12] >> I just want to let everybody know that
[01:22:14] the Parkside Commons has a new um
[01:22:17] >> new uh maintenance crew or property
[01:22:19] management crew as of last month.
[01:22:22] >> Okay,
[01:22:24] >> Commissioner.
[01:22:25] >> No, Councelor N, we're looking for a new
[01:22:26] director of operations. I think you just
[01:22:28] volunteered for a basement project.
[01:22:30] [laughter]
[01:22:32] >> Uh, we're requesting another appraisal.
[01:22:33] This one is for ongoing litigation.
[01:22:36] looking for an independent appraisal uh
[01:22:37] to take to court.
[01:22:40] >> And this does say trial ready, not trail
[01:22:43] ready. Is that correct?
[01:22:45] >> We're going to keep it indoors. Yeah,
[01:22:46] >> just checking.
[01:22:49] >> 46. We're requesting uh modification of
[01:22:51] the tax roles. This is uh you might
[01:22:53] remember that
[01:22:55] >> larger role amendment that we brought a
[01:22:56] few weeks ago with respect to railroad
[01:22:58] properties. Four of these in
[01:23:01] inadvertently included um billboards on
[01:23:03] railroad properties. So these should not
[01:23:05] be rule section seven. They should be
[01:23:06] roll section one returning to fully
[01:23:08] taxable railroad owned not railroad
[01:23:10] used.
[01:23:13] Finally 47 I think we'll go to executive
[01:23:15] session on that. Discuss pending
[01:23:16] settlement.
[01:23:16] >> Um and madam president given the time
[01:23:18] and the duration of our meeting I
[01:23:20] request that we do that on Monday.
[01:23:21] >> Okay.
[01:23:22] >> If that works for you and for
[01:23:23] corporation council.
[01:23:24] >> Great. Thank you.
[01:23:27] >> Um I'm all set. Thank you madam
[01:23:29] president.
[01:23:29] >> Thank you. Uh, councelor Joneser.
processing_notes: Parkside Commons appraisal relates to larger development project. Railroad tax correction follows a larger roll amendment from prior weeks -- four billboards on railroad property inadvertently classified as railroad-use (Section 7) instead of fully taxable (Section 1).
Segment 20 of 23 from Common Council Study Session, Wednesday March 18th 2026
Transcript corrections applied: CNS -> CNS Engineers; SU -> Syracuse University
